title: GKE Platform Engineering & GitOps Landing Zone
subtitle: Enterprise-grade GKE landing zone provisioned with Terraform and synchronized via ArgoCD GitOps
problem: Disjointed Kubernetes clusters across multiple developer domains with no standardized network policies, resource constraints, or synchronized deployment models — leading to cloud waste and security risk.
approach: Built a fully declarative landing zone: Terraform provisions the GCP Shared VPC, private GKE clusters, and Cloud Armor. ArgoCD bootstraps in a hub cluster to synchronize state across namespace branches using an App-of-Apps pattern.
challenges: IP exhaustion in GCP subnetworks under rapid pod scaling — resolved by migrating to GKE alias IP allocation with custom secondary ranges and tuning the cluster autoscaler.
solution: Isolated VPC networking boundaries, GKE Workload Identity for keyless access to Google services, and strict NetworkPolicy enforcement across namespaces.
results: Unified deployment model across multiple developer teams.
Cut environment provisioning time from days to minutes.
Enforced consistent compliance on secret injection and network isolation.
businessImpact: Faster, safer environment provisioning for every application team.
Reduced security review overhead via standardized, auditable network boundaries.

title: Kubernetes ML Platform: Vertex AI & Kubeflow
subtitle: Automated end-to-end machine learning pipeline hosting Triton inference on GKE
problem: Data science teams struggled to move experimental models into production. Containerization was manual, causing inconsistent cold starts and inference failures under load.
approach: Serverless Kubeflow pipeline integrated with Vertex AI. Standard container structures, automated GPU scheduling, and Triton Inference Server with dynamic batching.
challenges: Costly idle GPU capacity — mitigated with zero-scale node pools that cold-migrate idle models to storage.
solution: CI pipelines that build model images, trigger training, run evaluation checks, and roll Triton pods with the newest evaluated model.
results: Cut time-to-production for ML models from weeks to under an hour.
Lowered inference latency using Triton dynamic batching.
Improved GPU utilization with zero-scale cold pooling.
businessImpact: Data science teams ship models independently without platform-team bottlenecks.
GPU spend scales with actual usage instead of always-on reservations.

title: Multi-Environment Terraform Landing Zone
subtitle: Reusable Terraform module library for multi-environment GCP landing zones
problem: Each new environment (dev/stage/prod) was hand-built, causing drift, inconsistent IAM boundaries, and slow onboarding for new product squads.
approach: Designed a versioned module library (networking, IAM, GKE, Cloud SQL, secrets) consumed by thin per-environment root configs, with remote state and locking in GCS.
challenges: Coordinating module version upgrades across many environments without breaking existing state — solved with semantic versioning and a staged rollout process through non-prod first.
solution: A `platform/modules` library plus environment root configs that pin module versions, code-reviewed via pull request with automated `terraform plan` output posted as PR comments.
results: New environment bootstrap time dropped from days to under an hour.
Configuration drift across environments eliminated.
Consistent IAM and network policy enforced by default.
businessImpact: New product squads onboard onto compliant infrastructure without platform-team hand-holding.
Fewer security exceptions since every environment inherits the same guardrails.

title: Cloud-Native CI/CD Platform Pipeline
subtitle: Jenkins builds containerized services, Terraform defines GKE, Ansible + Istio deliver secured workloads
problem: Manual deployment scripts made releases slow and inconsistent across services, with no standard security scanning gate before production.
approach: A single pipeline template: Jenkins builds and scans container images, Terraform provisions the target GKE environment, Ansible configures node-level tooling, and Istio enforces mTLS between services.
challenges: Long build times from redundant dependency installs — fixed with container layer caching and self-hosted runner scale sets.
solution: Shared Jenkins library used by every service repo, with mandatory SAST/dependency scan stages and progressive canary rollout via Istio traffic splitting.
results: Cut the build-and-deploy cycle from hours to minutes.
Standardized security scanning across every service pipeline.
Canary rollouts reduced bad-deploy blast radius.
businessImpact: Multiple daily releases became routine instead of risky.
Security scanning gate caught vulnerable dependencies before production.

title: Cloud Armor & Istio Security Perimeter
subtitle: Layered edge and mesh security for internet-facing GKE workloads
problem: Internet-facing services had no consistent WAF layer or service-to-service authentication, leaving them exposed to L7 attacks and lateral movement inside the cluster.
approach: Cloud Armor security policies at the load balancer edge (rate limiting, OWASP rule sets, geo-fencing) combined with Istio mutual TLS and authorization policies inside the mesh.
challenges: Tuning Cloud Armor rules to avoid false positives on legitimate traffic — addressed with a staged preview mode before enforcing blocks.
solution: Defense-in-depth: Cloud Armor filters malicious traffic before it reaches the cluster, Istio enforces mTLS and namespace-scoped authorization policies between services.
results: Blocked a substantial volume of malicious edge traffic before it reached application pods.
All service-to-service traffic encrypted and authenticated by default.
Reduced blast radius of any single compromised service.
businessImpact: Meaningfully reduced exposure to common web attack patterns without slowing releases.
Simplified security audits with centrally defined, auditable policy.

title: ELK & Prometheus Observability Platform
subtitle: Centralized logging, metrics, and alerting across a multi-cluster GKE fleet
problem: Logs and metrics were scattered per cluster with no unified alerting, making incident triage slow and inconsistent.
approach: Centralized ELK stack for log aggregation, Prometheus + Grafana for metrics and dashboards, with alert routing standardized through a single on-call pipeline.
challenges: Log volume made storage costs balloon — addressed with index lifecycle management and sampling for low-value debug logs.
solution: Fluent Bit ships logs to a central Elasticsearch cluster; Prometheus federation aggregates metrics across clusters; Grafana dashboards and alert rules are version-controlled and deployed as code.
results: Cut mean time to detect production issues significantly.
One dashboard set for on-call, replacing scattered per-cluster tooling.
Reduced log storage costs via lifecycle policies.
businessImpact: Faster incident response reduced customer-facing downtime.
Predictable observability costs instead of runaway log storage spend.

title: Automated GCP Cost Optimization Engine
subtitle: Serverless orchestration that right-sizes compute, storage, and database spend
problem: Idle GPU VMs, oversized disks, and high-tier Cloud SQL instances stayed active outside business hours, causing significant avoidable monthly spend.
approach: A serverless scanner rates GCP resource activity using Cloud Logging and API metrics, then executes automated downsizing, snapshotting, or shutdown via Cloud Functions.
challenges: Avoiding accidental shutdown of resources developers forgot to tag correctly — solved with a Slack dry-run warning window before any automated action.
solution: Cloud Scheduler triggers Cloud Functions to snapshot idle disks, scale non-production GKE node pools to zero after hours, and demote sandbox Cloud SQL tiers.
results: Meaningful year-one cost reduction across non-production environments.
Full visibility into unutilized resources via a daily Slack digest.
Improved tagging compliance from the warning feedback loop.
businessImpact: Freed budget for platform investment instead of idle infrastructure.
Gave engineering leadership a clear, ongoing cost-visibility signal.
